chunks.text: Katherine Yelick uses supercomputing to solve big problems - EECS at Berkeley Prof. Katherine Yelick is featured in MIT’s EECS Connector online news page titled “Putting the pieces together” , an overview of her career. Prof. Yelick, who is an alumna of MIT, upon entering as a freshman was determined not to study computer science. But after taking one computer science class, to her surprise she enjoyed it. Today she gives lectures on “How to Save the World with Computers” and is associate lab director of the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Categories Diversity Faculty View Open Faculty Positions
